name: Cottage Cheese Protein Pancakes
method: In a large bowl whisk together the cottage cheese, eggs, milk, vanilla extract, maple syrup and melted butter until smooth
In a separate bowl mix the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and the whey protein
Gently fold the dry ingredients into the wet mix. A few lumps are fine
Carefully fold in the blueberries
Let the batter rest for 5 to 10 minutes
Heat a pan over medium-low heat with a little butter
Pour roughly a quarter of the mix per pancake
Cook until bubbles form and the edges set, then flip and cook for another 1 to 2 minutes
Stack them up, add butter, drizzle maple syrup and enjoy

name: Cheesy Crispy Smashed Potato Bites
method: Boil the potatoes for 15 to 20 minutes until soft
Line a baking tray with baking paper
Add small piles of cottage cheese, mozzarella and parmesan to the tray
Place the squashed potatoes on top, or squash them down with a glass
Spray with olive oil
Season with garlic powder, oregano, chilli flakes, salt and pepper
Bake at 200°C for 25 to 30 minutes until golden and crispy
Mix all sauce ingredients together in a bowl
Serve with the crispy potato bites

name: One Tray Red Thai Chicken Rice Bake
method: Add the rice, chicken, broccoli, peppers and green beans to a large baking dish
Add the curry paste, ginger, garlic and seasonings
Pour over the coconut milk and enough water to cover the rice
Mix well
Cover tightly with foil
Bake at 190°C for 45 minutes
Remove the foil and bake for a further 5 minutes
Garnish with coriander and chilli if using
Serve

name: Slow Cooker Creamy Chicken Orzo
method: Add the chicken, bolognese sauce, tomato purée, garlic purée, stock, herbs, salt and pepper to your slow cooker
Mix well
Cook on high for 3 to 4 hours, or low for 6 to 7
Shred the chicken
Add the orzo, cream cheese, mozzarella and frozen mixed veg
Mix again
Put the lid back on and cook on high for another 20 to 30 minutes, stirring once or twice, until the orzo is soft and everything is creamy
Serve

name: Garlic Parmesan Chicken Tenders
method: Crush the cornflakes into crumbs — not dust, you still want a bit of crunch
Season the chicken with salt, pepper, paprika and coriander if using
Dip the chicken into the egg, then coat in the crushed cornflakes
Air fry at 180°C for around 10 minutes until cooked through and crispy
Mix the melted butter, garlic purée and parmesan together
Toss the cooked chicken in the garlic parmesan butter
Serve

name: Salmon & Avocado Bowl
method: Flake the salmon into a bowl
Add the avocado, yoghurt, lemon juice and cucumber
Season with salt and pepper
Mix well
Add hot sauce or dill if you're feeling fancy

name: High Protein Carrot Cake
method: Preheat oven to 180°C
Add the oats, protein powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, baking powder and bicarbonate of soda to a bowl
Mix in the grated carrots, egg whites, almond milk, Greek yoghurt and flavouring
Mix until combined
Pour into a lined baking dish or cake tin
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until cooked through
Mix together the frosting ingredients and spread over once cooled
Optional: sprinkle a little sweetener over the top before serving

name: Slow Cooker Chicken Tikka Masala
method: Throw everything into the slow cooker except the cream and rice
Mix well
Cook on low for 6 to 7 hours or high for 3 to 4 hours
Stir through the double cream just before serving
Serve with rice
